title: His Majesty King Charles III and Finlandâs President Alexander Stubb explore Finnish bioeconomy innovations in London
content:
The New Wood: Building a Bio-Based Future exhibition showcases Finnish wood-based innovations that offer alternatives to fossil-based materials across packaging, textiles, chemicals, and even designer bathtubs.

Organised by the Finnish Forest Association, the New Wood exhibition was invited to London by the Circular Bioeconomy Alliance (CBA), whose goal is to accelerate the global transition to a climate-neutral bioeconomy based on the circular economy. The CBA was founded by The King, a long-time advocate of the environment and sustainable development.

Marc PalahÃ­, CEO of the Circular Bioeconomy Alliance, stated that bioeconomy solutions need visibility and concrete examples.

title: Youth Barometer: Work is the biggest cause of pressure and stress among young people
content:
The 2025 Youth Barometer, which examines the pressures and expectations of young people, indicates that finding a job is the number one concern among young people. Of the respondents, 70 per cent said they experienced pressures to get a job and of the nine different causes for pressures respondents selected it most often. In addition, 50% said they felt uncertain and insecure about getting a job.

âThe Youth Barometer was initiated back in the 1990s as a reaction to the ongoing recession and a concern about whether young people want to work anymore. The latest Youth Barometer repeats the message from previous barometers that employment is an important way for young people to engage in society,â says Konsta Happonen, Statistical Researcher at the Finnish Youth Resea
